news.title: A host of issues: World Cup focus turns to Morocco with costs and legacy topping agenda
news.lede: With just over 1,400 days to go until 2030 kicks off, the co-hosts are facing a series of challenges familiar to their South African predecessors. Morocco’s World Cup adventure came to a halt with their quarter-final defeat by France. But taking a break, even for a brief post-season summer, is no option for officials of the FRMF, the country’s football federation. The Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (Wafcon), in its newly expanded 16-team format, begins in Rabat on Sunday, a week after the World Cup final in New Jersey, and Morocco then hosts the Under-17 Women’s World Cup in the autumn.

news.title: How World Cup 2026 proved football has remarkable ability to cure all ills
news.lede: The trophy safely on display at the Spanish FA headquarters, England’s wounds no longer so raw, and the annual football calendar now into its miniscule vacuum, you’re left to reflect on the past six weeks and ponder whether the 2026 edition in the United States, Canada and Mexico was a resounding success or an unmitigated flop. This one really could have gone either way. The expansion from 32 teams to 48 had the potential for greater drama but also the risk of suffocating, of making the World Cup the one thing it should never be: tedious. Would North America buy into soccer in a way we hadn’t seen at USA 1994?

news.title: The 2026 World Cup final between Argentina and Spain broke all audience records in the United States
news.lede: The 2026 World Cup final between Argentina and Spain not only crowned the new world champion: it was also remembered as an unprecedented television phenomenon in the United States. The match played last Sunday, July 19, in New Jersey became the most-watched soccer game in the country's history, with an average of 63 million viewers across the various broadcasts, according to data published this Tuesday by Variety and the consulting firm Nielsen. Spain's triumph over Argentina achieved a historic audience thanks to the combination of the English- and Spanish-language broadcasts. According to the report (via TyC Sports), the Fox broadcast drew nearly 39 million viewers, while Telemundo and Peacock broadcasts added another 23.9 million, cementing a record figure for a soccer match on U.S. soil.

news.title: Gavi speaks up on the scuffle with Leandro Paredes after the World Cup Final
news.lede: Things got out of hand after Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Final on Sunday. Argentine player Leandro Paredes and Spanish youngster Pablo Gavi got into a post-match brawl and the video went viral and the Argentine midfielder faced immense criticism. Football fans asked for a strict punishment and ban for Paredes following the altercation, but newly minted World Champion, Gavi feels otherwise. Gavi does not think Leandro Paredes deserves to be suspendedIn videos that went viral after the 2026 World Cup Final match between Spain and Argentina, 32-year-old Leandro Paredes appears to throw Pablo Gavi to the ground after he got involved in a brief scuffle between Paredes and Spain's Eric Garcia..

news.title: FIFA confirms 3 countries to host historic 2030 World Cup
news.lede: The 2030 FIFA World Cup will make history as the first edition of the tournament to be staged across six countries on three continents, following the appointment of Morocco, Portugal and Spain as hosts and special centenary matches in Uruguay, Argentina and Paraguay, FIFA announced. The tournament hosts were officially confirmed after all 211 FIFA Member Associations approved the proposal during an Extraordinary FIFA Congress in December 2024, marking the conclusion of a bidding process launched in October 2023. Morocco, Portugal and Spain will jointly stage the bulk of the expanded 48-team tournament, hosting all but three of the competition’s 101 matches. Meanwhile, Uruguay, Argentina and Paraguay will each host one match to commemorate the centenary of the FIFA World Cup, which was first played in Uruguay in 1930.

news.title: What’s next for Canada’s Serie A stars after the 2026 World Cup?
news.lede: Jonathan David and Ismael Koné featured for Canada at the 2026 World Cup and face a very different future now that club football returns. Canada’s run to the World Cup Round of 16 has only heightened interest in the nation’s football future, while many supporters are also exploring new online casinos. Two Serie A players represented Canada in a tournament they co-hosted alongside Mexico and the USA and which ended with Spain’s victory over Argentina in a Final played in New York. Spain’s victory means they have now beaten Italy for the longest unbeaten streak in international football (38).

news.title: 'Dibu' hints at Argentina retirement after World Cup loss
news.lede: Emiliano ‘Dibu’ Martínez has opened the door to retiring from Argentina's national team following the World Cup final defeat to Spain, saying he will reflect on whether it is time to "step aside." Following Sunday's loss, the national team goalkeeper took to Instagram on Tuesday to question his future with the Albiceleste. "I dreamed we would win it again. I dreamed of bringing it back to Argentina and making history once more," the Aston Villa goalkeeper wrote on his personal account.

news.title: Colombia’s World Cup Run Sent Beer Sales Soaring Up to 460% at Some Stores
news.lede: Beer sales in Colombia during the World Cup soared. Colombia’s run at the 2026 FIFA World Cup did more than unite millions of fans behind the national team while it lasted — it also transformed shopping habits across the country, sending beer sales soaring and boosting demand for snacks, prepared foods, televisions, and even air conditioners. According to figures released by Colombian retail chain Olimpica S.A., every match played by the Colombian national team generated a significant spike in consumer spending compared with a typical day in June. The strongest growth was recorded in beer sales, which surged by as much as 460% during Colombia’s Round of 16 match.
